title: Introducing the Developer Knowledge API and MCP Server
snippet: Google is launching the Developer Knowledge API and MCP Server in public preview. This new toolset provides a canonical, machine-readable way for AI assistants and agentic platforms to search and retrieve up-to-date documentation across Firebase, Google Cloud, Android, and more. By using the official MCP server, developers can connect tools directly to Google’s documentation corpus, ensuring that AI-generated code and guidance are based on authoritative, real-time context.
ai_summary: Google has launched the Developer Knowledge API and MCP Server in public preview, allowing AI assistants to access up-to-date documentation across various Google platforms. This matters because it ensures that AI-generated code is based on reliable and current information, improving the quality of developer tools.

title: Access public data insights faster: Data Commons MCP is now hosted on Google Cloud
snippet: Data Commons has launched a free, hosted Model Context Protocol (MCP) service on Google Cloud Platform, eliminating the need for users to manage complex local server installations. This update simplifies connecting AI agents and the Gemini CLI to Data Commons, allowing Google to handle security, updates, and resource management while users query data natively.
ai_summary: Data Commons has introduced a free Model Context Protocol (MCP) service on Google Cloud, simplifying access to public data insights. This is significant as it removes the need for users to manage local servers, making it easier and more secure to connect AI agents to data.

title: Making Gemini CLI extensions easier to use
snippet: To simplify the user experience and prevent startup failures, the Gemini CLI has introduced structured extension settings that eliminate the need for manual environment variable configuration. This update enables extensions to automatically prompt users for required details during installation and securely stores sensitive information, such as API keys, directly in the system keychain. Users can now easily manage and override these configurations globally or per project using the new Gemini exte
ai_summary: The Gemini CLI has updated its extension settings to streamline user experience by removing manual configuration requirements. This change is important because it helps prevent startup failures and securely manages sensitive information, making it easier for users to work with extensions.

title: Conductor Update: Introducing Automated Reviews

title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
snippet: Google has introduced FunctionGemma, a specialized 270M parameter model designed to bring efficient, action-oriented AI experiences directly to mobile devices through on-device function calling. By leveraging Google AI Edge and LiteRT-LM, the model enables complex tasks—such as managing calendars, controlling device hardware, or executing specific game logic in the "Tiny Garden" demo—to be performed entirely offline with high speed and low latency. Available for testing in the Google AI Edge Gal
ai_summary: Google has launched FunctionGemma, a model that enables on-device function calling for efficient AI experiences on mobile devices. This is important as it allows complex tasks to be performed offline, enhancing speed and user experience without relying on internet connectivity.

title: Why OpenAI really shut down Sora
snippet: OpenAI's decision last week to shut down Sora, its AI video-generation tool, just six months after releasing it to the public raised immediate suspicions. The app had invited users to upload their own faces — so was this some kind of elaborate data grab?
